query_normalized: predicting molecular complexity with information theory chemistry
rationale: This paper applies information theory (rate-distortion theory) to molecular systems, which matches the user's core mechanism of using information theory to study molecular properties in chemistry/biology. It satisfies criterion (a) as same-mechanism evidence (information-theoretic analysis of molecular systems) and (b) as measuring information-theoretic variables on the molecular domain, even though it focuses on biological codes rather than general molecular complexity prediction.
